tier1_posts.text:
@Photomusicrock It's fine so long as you don't replace all the members at once. This was all resolved centuries ago.
https://t.co/q3pjqMBWpW
Just bragging about ruining the lives of innocent children.
Just admitting they enforce speech restrictions that should terrify innocent children. https://t.co/B5uftAd4jV

@smugjd @NicoPerrino And that has ensured that freedom of speech doesn't rot to nothing as technology changes. Again, if you don't like where 1A leads, suggest changes to it or be clear that you're suggesting massive changes in how 1A has been interpreted for decades.

@ValerioCapraro Humans make precisely this same mistake all the time for the same reason. For example, the "save one child or 100 frozen embryos" hypothetical. Does that mean they don't reason about morality?

@smugjd @NicoPerrino I would say no on 1 and yes on 2. It's just like if I hand you a book someone else wrote to explain my view on some political issue. I am using the book as a tool to help me speak, but the book itself is not my speech.
@smugjd @NicoPerrino And that's fine. If new technologies require new speech restrictions, the right thing to do is to amend or repeal 1A, not to pretend it doesn't mean what it says.

@ReviewsPossum I have thoughts I can't easily articulate all the time. Right now, I'm thinking about exactly what the screen on my phone looks like as I reply. I have no idea how to explain that in words.
@SwiftBovine @TheOnion Normally I'd agree with you, but I might not quite be willing to die for the right to distribute depictions of animal cruelty.
@grammarengli "Wi-Fi" has no full form. It's a brand name that neither means nor stands for anything. This has been confirmed by Phil Belanger, founder and first chairman of the Wi-Fi Alliance.
@SwiftBovine @TheOnion Distributing depictions of animal cruelty is protected.

probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 12%: compound stress now ELEVATED with two legs at threshold, sustained extreme fear, and crypto legislation stalling due to banking lobby. Base case increased from 40% to 42%: macro headwinds are real but institutional infrastructure continues building (OCC/FDIC FAQ, Ripple Prime expansion). Mid case reduced from 38% to 35%: legislation stalling and macro stress reduce probability of the catalyst chain needed for $8-20. Bull case reduced from 12% to 11%: macro environment is actively hostile to moonshot scenarios. RLUSD recovery is modestly positive but doesn't offset deteriorating macro picture.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Counter-thesis pressure is moderate-high: compound macro stress at ELEVATED, crypto legislation actively blocked by banking lobby, Fear & Greed at extreme fear (18), XRP price at $1.41 well below thesis base case trajectory. The infrastructure narrative is advancing but has not yet translated into on-chain activity or ODL volume visibility.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues incremental upgrades; no breakout displacement of alternatives, but 11,500-bank network inertia remains the strongest competitive moat in cross-border payments.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No significant new adoption signals; B2B Connect remains a niche product without the scale to threaten institutional settlement corridor thesis.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys (formerly Onyx/JPM Coin) continues processing significant intra-bank volumes; remains the most credible private network competitor for institutional settlement.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BIS Nexus represents the sovereign alternative to private bridge currencies — if central banks coordinate through Nexus for cross-border settlement, it directly undermines the XRP bridge currency thesis. No new deployment data but structural threat remains the highest among competitors.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: RLUSD distribution heavily Ethereum-native ($1.23B of $1.588B on ETH vs $355M on XRPL). Ondo expanding tokenized securities on Ethereum. Ethereum is capturing the institutional tokenization activity that the thesis assumes will flow to XRPL.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data remains completely opaque — now entering 2026 Q1 with zero public verification of the $20-30B annualized target; this is the single largest data gap threatening thesis credibility.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No hold time or velocity data available; if XRP is used purely as a pass-through bridge with sub-second hold times, token price appreciation requires massive volume increases that remain unverified.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Compound stress at ELEVATED with all three macro legs deteriorating; Fear & Greed at 18 signals broad risk-off positioning that suppresses institutional risk appetite for new infrastructure adoption.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: XRP at $1.41 is ~65% below recent highs, sustained extreme fear for multiple sessions, but institutional infrastructure buildout (ETFs, custody, regulatory) continues — this is not a 2022-style capitulation yet.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Current administration is clearly pro-crypto (SEC enforcement retreat, OCC/FDIC FAQ reversal); the risk is not reversal but legislative stalling due to banking lobby resistance, which is actively occurring.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.09% — elevated but stable. BOJ is the rate risk vector: if forced to hike aggressively to defend yen, JGB yield surge could trigger carry trade unwind with global liquidity implications.

bear_case.bear_narrative: CALL: INCREASE_MONITORING. No kill switch is triggered, but two are at WARNING — both related to the thesis's ability to prove itself rather than external threats disproving it. The core finding is structural: Ripple's own stablecoin lives 78% off-XRPL, and the single most important validation metric (ODL volume) is a suspicious absence classified by corporate selective disclosure behavior. Competitors are investing billions (Stripe $1.1B) while the thesis's primary evidence is unavailable. Compound stress is pre-loaded with Brent approaching $85 rapidly — one catalyst from ELEVATED. The thesis paradox holds: the same financial stress that could validate the need for alternative settlement infrastructure would simultaneously threaten the institutions positioned to adopt it. We don't know if XRP is being used for settlement at scale, and that is the most important thing we don't know.
